title: rosa communis var. lanceolata
intro: Rosa communis var. lanceolata, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. lanceolata include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, and its hips, which are the fruit of the rose, can be used to make jams and jellies, as well as being a rich source of vitamin C.
